Projects Blog About

19. Model Performance for Machine Learning

02 January 2021 - less than 1 min read time
Tags: Fast Campus ML

Source: Fast Campus, WikiPedia, WikiPedia

MSE(Mean Squared Error)

\[MSE = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n [y_i - \hat{f}(x_i)]^2\] 
n: size of data
MSE is from 0 to infinity
\[RMSE = \sqrt{\frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n [y_i - \hat{f}(x_i)]^2}\]
  • Difference between real output value and predicted output value.
  • When \(MSE\) is smaller than it is better, but be careful about overfitting.
  • Thus, we need to predict \(f\) to reduce validation set’s MSE smaller.

MAPE(mean absolute percentage error)

\[MAPE = \frac{100}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n|\frac{y_i-\hat{f}(x_i)}{y_i}| \space (y_i \neq 0)\] 
MAPE is from 0% ~ 100%
n:sample size
  • MAPE is percentage value.
  • When it is close to 0, better regression model performance

Accuracy

\[Accuracy = \frac{corrected\space data}{overall} = \frac{TP+TN}{TP+FN+FP+TN}\] \[Precision = \frac{corrected \space data}{predicted \space negative \space classes} = \frac{TN}{TN + FN}\] \[Recall = \frac{corrected \space negative \space data}{actual \space negative \space classes} = \frac{TN}{FP + TN}\] \[Specificity = \frac{corrected \space positive \space data}{actual \space positive \space classes} = \frac{TP}{TP+FN}\]

Mix Accuracy, precision, recall, specificity

G-means, F1 measure

\[G-mean = \sqrt{specificity * recall}\] \[F_1 measure = \frac{2}{\frac{1}{precision}+\frac{1}{recall}} = 2 * \frac{precision * recall}{precision + recall}\]

ROC curve, AUC

  • x-axis is specificity, y-axis is recall.
  • AUC is area of ROC curve

with by Mark Joonho Lee
